coverageNote: Limited coverage. Ranked on 30 golden-backed MN10 phase(s), 7 model(s), from run(s) 2026-07-01T10-11-40-333Z, 2026-07-01T17-39-07-313Z. The golden is partial (not every word is graded); Content is deterministic token-F1 (cannot reward paraphrase/enrichment); Semantic is an advisory LLM-judge score (judge=openai/gpt-4o-mini, not in the ranked total). Rankings will shift as coverage grows.

methodology.description: Ranked on rubric v2.1 only (mixing versions is a build failure). overallScore = gateFactor × (0.60·fidelity + 0.25·usability + 0.15·transitional-richness), averaged over the golden-backed phases of each model's SINGLE best run (highest mean overall) — NOT cherry-picked best-per-phase across runs; metrics are that run's, not summed. fidelity = 0.5·segmentation + 0.5·content, strict micro-F1 vs the golden. v2.1 (SUTTA-012): golden words a model DROPS are charged as misses (no survivorship bias), and content F1 is decomposed into contentPrecision/contentRecall. All ranked runs use the IDENTICAL harness pipeline (same fixed phases, same passes, no retrieval context) — scores are comparable to each other but NOT to the richer production pipeline. Ranking is driven by overallScore only; contentF1 is reference-width-sensitive (partial remediation: golden v2 widened senses; dictionary-attested ALTERNATE SEGMENTATIONS are still penalized — known limitation). CAVEAT: fidelity only scores words the golden covers; where the golden is partial, unscored model words are excluded — see paliWordCoverage and the per-run golden-diff.

reasons: gemma-3-27b: 4/30 phases (< 15 coverage floor) — insufficient coverage to rank

grounding.closedBookNote: Models receive ONLY the raw Pāli phrase — no dictionary or retrieval context at inference. The board measures parametric Pāli knowledge + analysis skill; the production pipeline additionally feeds dictionary grounding, so production output is richer than benchmark output.

sources.name: SuttaCentral canonical MN 10 (Mahāsaṅgīti; Sujato segmentation)
sources.role: source text, phase cuts, and the text-integrity gate (morphemes must reconstruct these exact surfaces)

sources.name: Digital Pāli Dictionary (DPD; compiled from PTS PED, CPD, traditional grammars)
sources.role: factual authority: roots, POS, attested senses. The golden is machine-verified against it; golden-v2 sense additions were restricted to VERBATIM DPD strings

sources.name: Golden reference (51 phrases)
sources.role: token-overlap reference for Content-F1 and context for the judge. Wording is NOT neutral — a known limitation; see the facts-vs-prose roadmap (SUTTA-013)

sources.name: Golden v2 curation (2026-07-02)
sources.authority: LLM-selected (Claude curator + adversarial skeptic) from DPD data, mechanical verbatim-membership guards, codex (OpenAI) plan review
sources.role: 13 context-vetted sense additions, 1 wrong-homonym removal, 3 tooltip corrections; full audit log in docs/benchmarks/golden-v2-apply-log.json

sources.name: Semantic judge (openai/gpt-4o-mini)
sources.authority: LLM, different family from the golden curator; self-judge guard on the board
sources.role: advisory meaning-level score; asymmetric rubric (hallucination ≤0.4); emits per-word hallucination flags and goldenSuspect golden-QA flags

knownCircularity: Golden PROSE is Claude-worded (dictionary-checked facts, non-neutral phrasing) — token overlap favors similar phrasing until the facts-vs-prose split lands

Golden-v2 curation and its adversarial verification were both Claude-family (selection bounded to verbatim DPD content); a cross-family check (grok, 2026-07-02) independently reviewed all 17 applied changes: 17/17 upheld, 0 reverts (docs/benchmarks/golden-v2-grok-verify.md)
The judge anchors on the golden, so golden errors propagate into Semantic until flagged via goldenSuspect
